description: Run the Muncie unsteady flow simulation and use a Python script to compute bed shear stress distributions from the HEC-RAS HDF output file (Muncie.p04.hdf). Identify the time step of peak total flow across the system and compute the bed shear stress (τ) for every cross-section at that time step using the formula τ = (γ * n^2 * V^2) / (k_n^2 * R^(1/3)) where γ = 62.4 lb/ft³, n = 0.035, and k_n = 1.486. Convert the results from lb/ft² to Pascals (1 lb/ft² = 47.88 Pa). Export a CSV file to /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/shear_stress_analysis.csv with columns for River_Station, Velocity, Hydraulic_Radius, and Shear_Stress_Pa. Save a summary text file to /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/shear_stress_summary.txt containing the total count of cross-sections, the River Station with the maximum shear stress and its value, and the mean shear stress across all sections.
